name: bob-the-buildooor
description: Standalone Cardano self-custody browser-extension wallet (CIP-30) built on @harmoniclabs/buildooor
comment: Exact-pinned versions (no ^): a key-handling extension minimizes supply-chain drift (CLAUDE.md §2). Bump deliberately. @harmoniclabs/buildooor: bump when the keepRelevant fix (PR#12) ships.
comment2: Supply-chain (T7.1): install scripts are blocked by default via .npmrc (ignore-scripts=true). NO package is allow-listed to run install scripts — Vite 8 uses rolldown (prebuilt native binding, no install script), so the former esbuild allow-entry is gone; every @trezor/* transitive native-build dep (protobufjs/stellar-sdk/usb/blake-hash/tiny-secp256k1) is explicitly DENIED (false) since none of it is bundled (Trezor runs popup-side on connect.trezor.io). After `npm ci`, run `npm run allow-scripts`. NOTE: ignore-scripts also blocks lifecycle/pre-post hooks (postinstall, pretest, …), so patch-package can't auto-run on install — instead it is embedded directly in the `dev`/`build`/`test` commands (idempotent, ~1s) so the patches/ always apply before those flows. To apply patches manually use `npm run patch`. The patch-package dev-tree carries audit advisories but is dev-only — `npm audit --omit=dev` stays clean.
